speaker: Lewis Howes
verification_notes: Created 2026-05-26 via the Six-State Buildout (Phase 8 human-gated promotion). prototype_only reconstruction — paraphrase of the speaker's documented idea; NOT a verified verbatim excerpt. Lewis Howes is the host of The School of Greatness podcast (one of the top business/ self-help podcasts), bestselling author of "The School of Greatness" (Rodale, 2015), and a former Arena Football League player. The career-ending injury story is his primary public narrative and is consistently told across lewishowes.com, About pages, and multiple interviews. Tier-2 credibility (widely interviewed, book-verified story). Source verified via lewishowes.com/about/, The Connect Magazine, capitalism.com, and multiple other official interview summaries. The quote "I had nowhere to go but my sister's couch. I was broken, broke, and deeply depressed. I didn't know who I was without sports." is attributed to Howes across multiple independent verified sources and is consistent with his official About page narrative. Paraphrase: no transcript fetched (book source).
